content.json:
# Contoso Bank SQL Audit & AI Behavior Analytics PoC

Three-layer database behaviour analytics over **Azure SQL** and **SQL Server on Azure VM**, with **90 days of history preloaded** (`SqlAuditPoC_CL`) so every panel is populated immediately.

> _"Who accessed which database records, when, from where — and was the behaviour normal or abnormal, even for privileged users?"_

parameters.name: UserName
parameters.label: User
parameters.query: SqlAuditPoC_CL | where isnotempty(UserName_s) | distinct UserName=UserName_s | order by UserName asc

parameters.name: DetectionName
parameters.label: Detection
parameters.query: union isfuzzy=true (SqlAuditPoC_CL | project DetectionName=DetectionName_s) | where isnotempty(DetectionName) and DetectionName != 'None' | distinct DetectionName | order by DetectionName asc

parameters.name: DatabaseName
parameters.label: Database
parameters.query: SqlAuditPoC_CL | where isnotempty(DatabaseName_s) | distinct DatabaseName=DatabaseName_s | order by DatabaseName asc

content.query: let A = union isfuzzy=true (SqlAuditPoC_CL | where isnotempty(EventTime_t) | where EventTime_t >= {TimeRange:start} | extend U=UserName_s, O=ObjectName_s, D=DetectionName_s, P=tobool(IsPrivilegedUser_b)), (SQLSecurityAuditEvents | where TimeGenerated >= {TimeRange:start} | extend U=ServerPrincipalName, O=ObjectName, D='', P=(tolower(ServerPrincipalName) in ('dba_user','privileged_admin','breakglass_admin'))); A | summarize TotalEvents=count(), Anomalies=countif(isnotempty(D) and D != 'None'), HighRisk=countif(D in ('DBA after-hours sensitive access','Break-glass account used','Suspicious DELETE on financial object','Permission escalation')), Privileged=countif(P and isnotempty(D) and D != 'None'), Sensitive=countif(O has 'SensitiveCustomerData'), BreakGlass=countif(tolower(U)=='breakglass_admin') | extend b = bag_pack('1 Audit events', TotalEvents, '2 Behaviour anomalies', Anomalies, '3 High-risk anomalies', HighRisk, '4 Privileged-user anomalies', Privileged, '5 Sensitive-data accesses', Sensitive, '6 Break-glass uses', BreakGlass) | mv-expand b | extend Metric=tostring(bag_keys(b)[0]) | extend Value=tolong(b[Metric]) | project Metric, Value | order by Metric asc

content.json:
## 2. Data Access Timeline & Layer 2 — Machine-Learning Anomaly Baseline

_The right-hand chart uses KQL machine learning (`series_decompose_anomalies`) to learn the normal daily query volume and flag statistical outliers — no fixed thresholds._

content.query: SqlAuditPoC_CL | where isnotempty(EventTime_t) | where EventTime_t >= {TimeRange:start} | summarize Events=count() by bin(EventTime_t, 1d), SourceType=SourceType_s | render timechart

SqlAuditPoC_CL | where isnotempty(EventTime_t) and Action_s == 'BATCH COMPLETED' | make-series Queries=count() default=0 on EventTime_t from {TimeRange:start} to endofday(now()) step 1d | extend (anomalies, score, baseline) = series_decompose_anomalies(Queries, 1.5) | render anomalychart with(anomalycolumns=anomalies)

let priv = dynamic(['dba_user','privileged_admin','breakglass_admin']); let A = union isfuzzy=true (SqlAuditPoC_CL | where isnotempty(EventTime_t) | where EventTime_t >= {TimeRange:start} | extend U=UserName_s, O=ObjectName_s), (SQLSecurityAuditEvents | where TimeGenerated >= {TimeRange:start} | extend U=ServerPrincipalName, O=ObjectName); A | where isnotempty(U) | summarize Events=count(), DistinctObjects=dcount(O) by U | extend Class=iff(tolower(U) in (priv),'Privileged','Standard') | top 20 by Events desc

let priv = dynamic(['dba_user','privileged_admin','breakglass_admin']); union isfuzzy=true (SqlAuditPoC_CL | where isnotempty(EventTime_t) | where EventTime_t >= {TimeRange:start} | extend U=UserName_s), (SQLSecurityAuditEvents | where TimeGenerated >= {TimeRange:start} | extend U=ServerPrincipalName) | where isnotempty(U) | summarize Events=count() by Class=iff(tolower(U) in (priv),'Privileged','Standard') | render piechart

SqlAuditPoC_CL | where isnotempty(EventTime_t) | where EventTime_t >= {TimeRange:start} | extend U=UserName_s, O=ObjectName_s, S=Statement_s, DB=DatabaseName_s | where O has 'SensitiveCustomerData' or S has_any ('SensitiveCustomerData','SalaryBand','CreditScore','InternalRiskComment','VIPFlag') | project EventTime=EventTime_t, User=U, DatabaseName=DB, ObjectName=O, Statement=S | order by EventTime desc | take 100

content.json:
> **Layer 3 — AI Analyst.** Azure OpenAI reads the flagged audit evidence (read-only) and explains **why** each behaviour is suspicious and what to do next — grounded strictly in the records, never invented. Example findings from the current data:

**🔴 DBA after-hours sensitive access — `dba_user`**  
_Finding:_ `dba_user` read VIP salary / credit / risk fields in `auditdemo.SensitiveCustomerData` at ~22:00 UTC, outside the expected 08–18 window. Privileged access is allowed, but the timing and VIP-data target make this unusual for this user.  
_Evidence:_ after-hours; `auditdemo.SensitiveCustomerData`; SalaryBand / CreditScore / InternalRiskComment; RiskCategory=SensitiveDataAccess.  
_Recommended action:_ confirm an approved maintenance window; compare to peer DBAs; check whether VIP fields were required.

**🔴 Break-glass account used — `breakglass_admin`**  
_Finding:_ `breakglass_admin` was used to read `admin.AccessRequests`. Break-glass accounts should only be used during an approved incident.  
_Evidence:_ `admin.AccessRequests`; RiskCategory=BreakGlass.  
_Recommended action:_ verify an approved incident record exists; rotate break-glass credentials after use.

**🔴 Suspicious DELETE on financial object — `suspicious_user`**  
_Finding:_ `suspicious_user` issued a `DELETE` against `payments.WireTransfers`. Destructive statements against money-movement data are high risk.  
_Evidence:_ Statement includes DELETE; `payments.WireTransfers`; RiskCategory=DataModification.  
_Recommended action:_ confirm change ticket; validate the targeted rows; review the account entitlements.

_These narratives are AI-generated and preloaded, so the panel is demo-ready with no script to run. To regenerate live against the newest anomalies, run `./scripts/run-ai-analysis.ps1`._

content.query: SqlAuditPoC_CL | where isnotempty(EventTime_t) | where EventTime_t >= {TimeRange:start} | where isnotempty(DetectionName_s) and DetectionName_s != 'None' | project EventTime=EventTime_t, UserName=UserName_s, ObjectName=ObjectName_s, DetectionName=DetectionName_s, RiskCategory=RiskCategory_s, AnomalyScore=toint(AnomalyScore_d), ['AI signals']=BehaviorExplanation_s | order by AnomalyScore desc | take 25

content.json:
## 6. Layer 1 — Deterministic Rule Detections

_Fixed, explainable KQL rules (e.g. break-glass used, after-hours sensitive access, DELETE on financial objects). Each tile is a rule that either fires or doesn't._

content.query: let A = union isfuzzy=true (SqlAuditPoC_CL | where isnotempty(EventTime_t) | where EventTime_t >= {TimeRange:start} | extend D=DetectionName_s), (SQLSecurityAuditEvents | where TimeGenerated >= {TimeRange:start} | extend D=''); let t = (name:string) { A | where D == name | summarize C=count() | extend Detection=name }; union (t('DBA after-hours sensitive access')), (t('Break-glass account used')), (t('Suspicious DELETE on financial object')), (t('Permission escalation')), (t('Query volume anomaly')), (t('Out-of-role data access')) | project Detection, C | order by C desc

SqlAuditPoC_CL | where isnotempty(EventTime_t) | where EventTime_t >= {TimeRange:start} | extend EventTime=EventTime_t, SourceType=SourceType_s, UserName=UserName_s, DatabaseName=DatabaseName_s, ObjectName=ObjectName_s, Action=Action_s, DetectionName=DetectionName_s, RiskCategory=RiskCategory_s, AnomalyScore=toint(AnomalyScore_d), BehaviorExplanation=BehaviorExplanation_s, Statement=Statement_s, ClientIp=ClientIp_s | extend IsAnomaly=(isnotempty(DetectionName) and DetectionName != 'None') | where UserName in ({UserName}) | where SourceType in ({SourceType}) | where RiskCategory in ({RiskCategory}) | where DatabaseName in ({DatabaseName}) | where DetectionName in ({DetectionName}) | where '{ShowOnlyAnomalies}' == '0' or IsAnomaly | project EventTime, SourceType, UserName, DatabaseName, ObjectName, Action, DetectionName, RiskCategory, AnomalyScore, BehaviorExplanation, Statement | order by AnomalyScore desc, EventTime desc | take 500

union isfuzzy=true (BehaviorAnalytics | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | summarize InvestigationPriority=max(InvestigationPriority), Activities=count() by User=UserPrincipalName | order by InvestigationPriority desc | take 50), (datatable(User:string, Info:string)['(no UEBA data)','UEBA context is optional — it requires Microsoft Sentinel UEBA (BehaviorAnalytics table), which is not populated in this workspace.'])

content.json:
**Three detection layers in this PoC:**  **Layer 1 — Deterministic rules** (section 6): fixed, explainable KQL rules.  **Layer 2 — Machine learning** (section 2, right chart): `series_decompose_anomalies` learns each user/system's normal behaviour and flags statistical outliers — not a fixed rule.  **Layer 3 — AI** (section 5): Azure OpenAI explains *why* the flagged behaviour is suspicious, grounded strictly in the audit evidence.

**Deploy:** `azd up` (preloads 90-day history)  ·  **Live VM scenarios:** `./scripts/run-poc-scenarios.ps1 -Setup` then `./scripts/generate-wow-detections.ps1`  ·  **AI:** `./scripts/run-ai-analysis.ps1`

**Demo order:** Executive Overview → Timeline & ML baseline → Layer 1 rule detections → Investigation View (sort by AnomalyScore) → Layer 3 AI findings. Set the **Time range** to 30 days.
